title: UMN TMCs: Fresh Frozen Tissue Protocol
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"8cegl","text":"Protocol from BLS Histology at University of Minnesota for processing snap frozen prospective surgically obtained tissue. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"dh554","text":"This protocol required prior approval by the users' local Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC), Institutional Review Board (IRB) or equivalent ethics committee(s), depending on the source of the tissue.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"aqufr","text":"Prepare a liquid nitrogen bath large enough to hold a cryovial","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Tissue is received from surgical team and cut to a smaller size, ~1g
Tissue is placed in PBS buffer
When ready to freeze sample, remove tissue from PBS buffer and tap off excess buffer with a kimwipe
Tissue is placed in appropriately labeled cryovial
Snap freeze the cryovial in liquid nitrogen and store at -80°C
creator: Allie Abdellatif
